content.problemStatement:
NNN people (numbered 111 through NNN) are trying to catch Chef because he has a secret recipe for the most delicious biryani ever. Chef does not have a number.

The chase starts at time t=0t = 0t=0 and takes place on a circular road with length LLL. Positions on the road are described by one real-valued coordinate; for each real number xxx, position x+Lx+Lx+L is the same as position xxx. Chef's initial position is Xc=0X_c = 0Xc​=0 and for each valid iii, the initial position of the iii-th person is XiX_iXi​.

Chef's maximum velocity is VcV_cVc​ and for each valid iii, the maximum velocity of the iii-th person is ViV_iVi​. At each moment in time, any person (including Chef) with maximum velocity VVV may choose any velocity vvv (a real number) between −V-V−V and VVV inclusive, possibly different at different moments in time, independently from all other people and this person's velocity at all other moments in time. All people move continuously — for any very small time difference Δt\Delta tΔt, a person at a position xxx and with a velocity vvv at time ttt is approximately at the position x+v⋅Δtx + v \cdot \Delta tx+v⋅Δt at time t+Δtt+\Delta tt+Δt.

When Chef is at the same position as one of the NNN people at the same moment in time, he is considered caught and the chase ends. It can be proved that Chef is unable to keep escaping forever; formally, there is a real number TuT_uTu​ such that Chef can always be caught at a finite time which does not exceed TuT_uTu​, regardless of his choices.

The NNN people want to catch Chef at the smallest possible time, while Chef wants to maximise the time when he will be caught. Find the time at which Chef gets caught if all people act optimally.
constraints: 1≤Xi≤L−11 \le X_i \le L-11≤Xi​≤L−1 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, LLL and VcV_cVc​.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,V2,…,VNV_1, V_2, \ldots, V_NV1​,V2​,…,VN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number — the time when Chef gets caught. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute or relative error does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.
